data.name: about.manifesto_excerpt
data.value: wInstaller should feel like something a first-party disk utility would be if it actually explained itself. No telemetry, no uploads, no vague "optimizing" language — just a local assistant that tells you what it found, what it's about to do, and why.

data.name: about.tagline
data.value: A calm, native assistant for building bootable Windows and Linux USB installers — on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

data.name: analyze-usb.subtitle
data.value: wInstaller plans every command as arguments and marks destructive steps explicitly.

data.name: choose-iso.subtitle
data.value: Select installation media. The importer rejects non-ISO files, then wInstaller mounts it read-only to analyze.

data.name: confirm-erase.simulate_body
data.value: Run the full sequence without erasing anything.

data.name: confirm-erase.subtitle
data.value: Type the exact USB drive name to unlock the destructive operation.

data.name: confirm-erase.warning
data.value: All data on {drive_name} will be erased.

data.name: create-usb.subtitle_real
data.value: wInstaller is preparing the drive, copying files, and validating boot media.

data.name: create-usb.subtitle_simulate
data.value: Dry-run mode: wInstaller runs the full checklist without executing any destructive command.

data.name: done.subtitle_real
data.value: The installer USB was created, validated, and safely ejected.

data.name: done.subtitle_simulate
data.value: The simulated checklist completed. Turn off Simulate to create real media.

data.name: insert-usb.empty_message
data.value: Plug in a USB drive, then choose Refresh. Internal disks are never shown.

data.name: insert-usb.subtitle
data.value: Internal disks are hidden and refused by the planning engine.

data.name: use-with-vmware.license_reminder
data.value: Windows may require a valid license.

data.name: use-with-vmware.not_installed_message
data.value: You can still use the USB you created. Install a virtualization app to continue in a VM.

data.name: use-with-vmware.not_installed_title
data.value: No supported virtualization app is installed

data.name: use-with-vmware.subtitle
data.value: wInstaller does not create or start virtual machines without explicit user intent.

data.name: verify-iso.subtitle
data.value: wInstaller explains what it detected before a USB drive is selected.

data.name: welcome.info_confirmation_body
data.value: Erase is blocked until the drive name is typed.

data.name: welcome.info_iso_body
data.value: Choose Windows or Linux installation media.

data.name: welcome.info_local_body
data.value: Every operation runs on this machine. No uploads.

data.name: welcome.info_usb_body
data.value: Use removable media with enough capacity.

data.name: welcome.subtitle
data.value: wInstaller guides ISO selection, USB verification, erase confirmation, copy planning, validation, and virtualization handoff.
